tactical_shape: Better team in the opening game; controlled enough to keep South Africa under pressure
Lost the ball in midfield before South Africa's goal, suggesting a midfield turnover rather than a stated formation
standout_players: Óscar Pérez was beaten for Tshabalala's goal
Rafael Márquez equalised with 11 minutes left
Mexico were described as the better team and created the disallowed goal / early pressure
home_read.momentum: Started as the stronger side and still rescued a point, but the draw felt like a missed chance after being unable to hold the lead South Africa briefly took.

tactical_shape: Began the game tightly and awkwardly
After the break, regained possession in midfield and scored through three slick passes into a left-channel attack
Celebratory, high-energy, collective approach rather than a detailed formation
standout_players: Itumeleng Khune kept Mexico out with brilliant goalkeeping
Kagisho Dikgacoi played the defence-splitting pass for Tshabalala's goal
Siphiwe Tshabalala scored the opener with a left-footed finish
Katlego Mphela hit the woodwork late
Rafael Márquez's back-post equaliser denied South Africa the win
away_read.momentum: The opening result created a huge burst of national belief and joy, even if the team later faded in the tournament.
